---
title: "Green-and-white Sedge (Carex longii)"
slug: "carex-longii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/565849555/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Green-and-white Sedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/565849555/medium.jpg" alt="Green-and-white Sedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/joeysalerno12">
joeysalerno12</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/313341609">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=76073)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in wet areas, such as ditches, marshes, and disturbed sites.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing, succulent herb characterized by its fleshy leaves and small white to pale pink three-petaled flowers. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub and sandhills, often in open areas with sparse vegetation. It grows during the warmer months and can form small patches on the ground, sometimes spreading vegetatively.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/76073-Carex-longii)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/carex-longii/)
